comment_text.sn7.10:1.1: Contrast this vivid description of the woes of the rural life with the satisfaction of the wealthy rancher at Snp 1.2.
comment_text.sn7.10:5.2: For santhāra as “mat” or “carpet” (rather than “blanket”) see SN 35.243:2.1, AN 6.16:2.5, AN 3.35:2.1, AN 3.126:3.7, etc.
comment_text.sn7.10:6.3: All of whom would have been dependent on their father.
comment_text.sn7.10:9.1: The Buddha appropriates and transforms the brahmin’s accusations, an early example of reclaiming language to see the “glass half full”.

root_text.sn7.10:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosalesu viharati aññatarasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe.
root_text.sn7.10:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa bhāradvājagottassa brāhmaṇassa catuddasa balībaddā naṭṭhā honti.
root_text.sn7.10:1.3: Atha kho bhāradvājagotto brāhmaṇo te balībadde gavesanto yena so vanasaṇḍo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā addasa bhagavantaṁ tasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe nisinnaṁ pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
root_text.sn7.10:1.4: Disvāna yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavato santike imā gāthāyo abhāsi:
root_text.sn7.10:16.1: Evaṁ vutte, bhāradvājagotto brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.10:16.3: Seyyathāpi, bho gotama, nikkujjitaṁ vā ukkujjeyya, paṭicchannaṁ vā vivareyya, mūḷhassa vā maggaṁ ācikkheyya, andhakāre vā telapajjotaṁ dhāreyya: ‘cakkhumanto rūpāni dakkhantī’ti; evameva bhotā gotamena anekapariyāyena dhammo pakāsito.
root_text.sn7.10:16.4: Esāhaṁ bhavantaṁ gotamaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāmi dhammañca bhikkhusaṅghañca.
root_text.sn7.10:16.5: Labheyyāhaṁ bhoto gotamassa santike pabbajjaṁ, labheyyaṁ upasampadan”ti.
root_text.sn7.10:17.1: Alattha kho bhāradvājagotto brāhmaṇo bhagavato santike pabbajjaṁ, alattha upasampadaṁ.
root_text.sn7.10:17.2: Acirūpasampanno panāyasmā bhāradvājo eko vūpakaṭṭho appamatto ātāpī pahitatto viharanto nacirasseva—yassatthāya kulaputtā sammadeva agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajanti, tadanuttaraṁ—brahmacariyapariyosānaṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja vihāsi.
root_text.sn7.10:17.3: “Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā”ti abbhaññāsi.
root_text.sn7.10:17.4: Aññataro ca panāyasmā bhāradvājo arahataṁ ahosīti.

translation_text.sn7.10:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Kosalans in a certain forest grove.
translation_text.sn7.10:1.2: Now at that time one of the brahmins of the Bhāradvāja clan had lost fourteen oxen.
translation_text.sn7.10:1.3: While looking for them he went to that forest grove, where he saw the Buddha sitting down cross-legged, his body set straight, and mindfulness established in his presence.
translation_text.sn7.10:1.4: He went up to the Buddha, and recited these verses in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn7.10:2.3: missing for the past six days:
translation_text.sn7.10:2.4: that’s why this ascetic is happy.
translation_text.sn7.10:3.3: with just one or two leaves:
translation_text.sn7.10:3.4: that’s why this ascetic is happy.
translation_text.sn7.10:4.4: that’s why this ascetic is happy.
translation_text.sn7.10:5.4: that’s why this ascetic is happy.
translation_text.sn7.10:6.3: with one or two children each:
translation_text.sn7.10:6.4: that’s why this ascetic is happy.
translation_text.sn7.10:7.2: a wife with sallow, blotchy skin
translation_text.sn7.10:7.3: to wake him with a kick:
translation_text.sn7.10:7.4: that’s why this ascetic is happy.
translation_text.sn7.10:8.4: that’s why this ascetic is happy.”
translation_text.sn7.10:9.3: missing for the past six days:
translation_text.sn7.10:10.1: You’re right, brahmin, I don’t have
translation_text.sn7.10:10.3: with just one or two leaves:
translation_text.sn7.10:11.1: You’re right, brahmin, I don’t have
translation_text.sn7.10:12.1: You’re right, brahmin, I don’t have
translation_text.sn7.10:13.1: You’re right, brahmin, I don’t have
translation_text.sn7.10:13.3: with one or two children each:
translation_text.sn7.10:14.1: You’re right, brahmin, I don’t have
translation_text.sn7.10:14.2: a wife with blotchy, pockmarked skin
translation_text.sn7.10:14.3: to wake me up with a kick:
translation_text.sn7.10:15.1: You’re right, brahmin, I don’t have
translation_text.sn7.10:16.1: When he had spoken, the brahmin said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn7.10:16.3: As if he were righting the overturned, or revealing the hidden, or pointing out the path to the lost, or lighting a lamp in the dark so people with clear eyes can see what’s there, worthy Gotama has made the teaching clear in many ways.
translation_text.sn7.10:16.4: I go for refuge to the worthy Gotama, to the teaching, and to the mendicant Saṅgha.
translation_text.sn7.10:16.5: May I receive the going forth, the ordination in the worthy Gotama’s presence?”
translation_text.sn7.10:17.1: And the brahmin received the going forth, the ordination in the Buddha’s presence.
translation_text.sn7.10:17.2: Not long after his ordination, Venerable Bhāradvāja, living alone, withdrawn, diligent, keen, and resolute, soon realized the supreme end of the spiritual path in this very life. He lived having achieved with his own insight the goal for which gentlemen rightly go forth from the lay life to homelessness.
translation_text.sn7.10:17.3: He understood: “Rebirth is ended; the spiritual journey has been completed; what had to be done has been done; there is nothing further for this place.”
translation_text.sn7.10:17.4: And Venerable Bhāradvāja became one of the perfected.

variant_text.sn7.10:0.3: Bahudhītarasutta → bahudhītusuttaṁ (bj, pts2ed); bahudhitisuttaṁ (bj, sya1ed, sya2ed); bahudhītisuttaṁ (cck); bahudhīti (pts1ed)
variant_text.sn7.10:2.1: Na hi → naha (bj, sya-all, km, pts2ed)
